entries.mem0ai/mem0: A universal memory layer providing multi-level storage for users, sessions, and autonomous states. It differentiates itself with temporal reasoning for time-aware ranking and hybrid retrieval combining semantic, keyword, and entity signals.
entries.volcengine/OpenViking: An open-source context database that treats agent memory as a filesystem using viking:// URIs. It utilizes a tiered L0/L1/L2 loading system to significantly reduce token consumption while improving task completion rates.
entries.supermemoryai/supermemory: A high-performance memory engine featuring a custom vector graph and sub-300ms recall for massive token volumes. It offers native connectors for productivity tools like Notion and Google Drive to maintain auto-updating user profiles.
entries.garrytan/gbrain: An opinionated brain for Hermes Agents that automatically extracts typed entity relationships into a self-wiring knowledge graph. It is designed for multi-tenant company deployments to synthesize cited answers across people and meeting notes.
entries.vectorize-io/hindsight: A learning-centric memory system that implements specific retain, recall, and reflect workflows for long-term knowledge. It provides an easy-to-integrate LLM wrapper with enterprise storage support for PostgreSQL and Oracle AI Database.
entries.campfirein/byterover-cli: A unique memory provider that manages context trees as git repositories, allowing for branching, merging, and versioning of markdown files. It features a high-speed 5-tier retrieval system with sub-100ms latency.
entries.plastic-labs/honcho: A stateful agent library that focuses on extracting reasoning-based conclusions from conversations rather than simple vector matching. It tracks evolving entities across users, groups, and projects using hybrid BM25 and vector search.
entries.greyhaven-ai/autocontext: A recursive self-improving harness that uses a five-role architecture to manage strategy and curation. It helps agents succeed on complex tasks by producing reusable playbooks and detailed execution traces.
entries.stephenschoettler/hermes-lcm: A lossless context management plugin utilizing a hierarchical DAG structure for depth-aware compaction. It ensures no message is lost by combining a SQLite-backed raw history store with targeted retrieval tools.
entries.elkimek/honcho-self-hosted: A self-hosted backend for the Honcho library that enables private, cross-session persistence for Hermes Agent. It supports local inference servers and OpenAI-compatible providers via a Docker-based setup.
entries.yoloshii/ClawMem: A local-first memory engine that operates entirely on-device without cloud dependencies or API keys. It integrates via MCP servers and Claude Code hooks using a mix of BM25, vector search, and knowledge graphs.
entries.EfficientContext/ContextPilot: A context optimization tool that accelerates LLM inference by reordering and deduplicating shared context blocks. It boosts prefill throughput by 1.5–3× while maintaining accuracy across SGLang, vLLM, and Hermes Agent.
entries.plur-ai/plur: A shared memory layer for multi-agent systems that stores data as open-format YAML engrams. It employs ACT-R activation to strengthen relevant facts and decay stale information over time.
entries.keepnotes-ai/keep: A reflective memory system featuring automated indexing for local directories, URLs, and multimedia. It uses ranked graph traversal and edge tags to create navigable relationships between pieces of information.
entries.AxDSan/mnemosyne: A zero-dependency memory system offering sub-millisecond latency through local SQLite storage. Its MEMORIA engine performs structured fact extraction and recursive gap analysis for high-speed retrieval.
entries.yepyhun/Brainstack: A memory kernel that prevents 'flat memory blob' issues by using a five-shelf architecture. It consolidates temporal continuity, graph truth, and corpus retrieval into a single plugin using SQLite and Kuzu.
entries.yantrikos/yantrikdb-hermes-plugin: A self-maintaining plugin that provides explainable retrieval by tagging every recall with a 'why_retrieved' reason. It natively tracks contradictions and manages agent-authored skills via an outcome ledger.
entries.Ladybug-Memory/hermes-memory-plugin: A local-only plugin using a columnar .lbdb embedded graph database. It prioritizes the most critical information using a 1-10 importance ranking system during context prefetch.
entries.amanning3390/flowstate-qmd: An anticipatory memory system that prefetches context by analyzing agent session logs. It is an MCP-native server using SQLite-vec and local GGUF models for proactive RAG.
entries.MukundaKatta/hermes-agentmemory: A pull-model episodic memory plugin that ensures true deletion of records to prevent outdated summaries from affecting recall. It includes a trace.jsonl audit log for full transparency of memory operations.
entries.alias8818/hermes-tool-slimmer: A utility designed to reduce tool-schema token overhead through BM25 ranking and keyword selection. It optimizes how Hermes Agent handles native tools and MCP integrations to save context space.

entries.mukul975/Anthropic-Cybersecurity-Skills: This repository provides 754 structured cybersecurity skills mapped to major frameworks like MITRE ATT&CK and NIST. It enables Hermes to perform sub-second AI skill discovery across 26 specialized security domains using the agentskills.io standard.
entries.AMAP-ML/SkillClaw: SkillClaw uses an agentic evolver to automate skill deduplication and quality improvement based on interaction history. It unifies skill libraries across multiple devices and natively supports Hermes and Claude Code integrations.
entries.conorbronsdon/avoid-ai-writing: This tool audits and rewrites content to eliminate detectable AI writing patterns by identifying 42 distinct structural tells. It utilizes a tiered word replacement table and a second-pass audit to ensure natural, plain-language output.
entries.wondelai/skills: A modular, cross-platform library that provides Hermes with frameworks for architecture, UX design, and product strategy. It features a dedicated CLI for easy installation and full compatibility with agentskills.io-compliant agents.
entries.DougTrajano/pydantic-ai-skills: This project implements the Agent Skills open specification for Pydantic AI with a focus on type safety. It reduces token consumption through progressive disclosure of skill metadata and supports both filesystem and programmatic definitions.
entries.Agents365-ai/drawio-skill: This skill allows agents to generate complex draw.io diagrams from natural language descriptions with built-in presets for UML and ML models. It includes a self-check visual feedback loop to automatically fix layout issues during the export process.
entries.Cranot/super-hermes: Super-hermes teaches agents to write their own analytical prompts and custom thinking instructions for complex problem-solving. It is designed to identify structural conservation laws in codebases and generate reports on analysis blind spots.
entries.smartcontractkit/chainlink-agent-skills: This library enables Hermes to interact with decentralized oracle networks for CCIP, VRF, and data feed operations. It supports cross-chain identity management and automated proof of reserve verification within agent-led workflows.
entries.tlehman/litprog-skill: This skill implements literate programming by weaving source code, prose, and diagrams into unified .lit.md files. It features a tangler to extract runnable code and automatically syncs edits between source files and documentation.
entries.esaradev/icarus-plugin: Icarus provides cross-instance shared memory via markdown-based directories to help agents remember their work over time. It includes an end-to-end pipeline for extracting decision-quality training data to fine-tune replacement models.
entries.chigwell/skilldock.io: Skilldock is a registry for reusable AI skills that features an OpenAPI-driven SDK and CLI for lifecycle management. It uniquely supports skill monetization through Stripe and the TON blockchain while handling automated dependency resolution.
entries.black-forest-labs/skills: These official skills provide standardized prompting guidelines and API integration patterns for the FLUX image generation model family. They enable Hermes to use precise hex color specifications and structured JSON prompting for high-fidelity visual output.
entries.Romanescu11/hermes-skill-factory: This meta-skill plugin monitors active workflows to automatically detect and propose new, reusable skills. It generates both Python scaffolds and SKILL.md documentation, allowing the agent to expand its own capabilities via slash commands.
entries.ReinaMacCredy/maestro: Maestro is a local-first harness that manages long-running agent tasks using on-disk state files instead of chat history. It facilitates a structured spec-to-ship workflow and uses passive handoff envelopes for seamless task resumption.
entries.tiann/execplan-skill: This skill focuses on the autonomous execution of complex, multi-step tasks with integrated checkpoints. It provides real-time progress tracking and built-in rollback procedures to ensure safe recovery from execution failures.
entries.cablate/Agentic-MCP-Skill: This progressive MCP client uses a three-layer lazy loading pattern to significantly reduce context token usage. It maintains persistent server connections via a socket-based daemon and supports hot-reloading for dynamic configuration updates.
entries.armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge: Skill-forge converts repositories and developer discourse into version-pinned, agentskills.io-compliant instructions. It automates the creation process through Brief, Create, and Test workflows while maintaining verifiable file-and-line provenance.
entries.amanning3390/hermeshub: Hermeshub is a community marketplace for browsing and installing skills with integrated security scanning and threat detection. It features an agent-to-agent feedback system and supports creator payments via crypto or Stripe.
entries.PederHP/skillsdotnet: This C# and .NET implementation of the Agent Skills specification integrates with Microsoft.Extensions.AI for tool-based loading. It supports progressive disclosure via SKILL.md files and provides automatic registration for common skill directories.
entries.adnw-vinc/hermes-nextcloud: This skill enables Hermes to manage files, calendars, tasks, and contacts through standard WebDAV and CalDAV protocols. It ensures secure communication via Nextcloud App Passwords and includes a guided setup script for easy configuration.

entries.numtide/llm-agents.nix: This project provides daily updated Nix packages and Flakes for instant execution of AI coding agents, including Hermes. It is ideal for developers seeking a reproducible, terminal-based environment that supports multi-agent orchestrators without manual installation.
entries.TheAiSingularity/hermesclaw: Focusing on high-security environments, this project implements hardware-enforced sandboxing using NVIDIA OpenShell kernel-level security. It offers multi-layer protection with pre-configured policy presets to strictly control network egress and filesystem access during agent execution.
entries.0xrsydn/nix-hermes-agent: This repository offers a NixOS module for declarative management of Hermes configurations, systemd services, and secure secrets via sops-nix. It bridges the gap between reproducibility and flexibility by supporting both Nix-managed skills and interactive CLI installations.
entries.rookiemann/portable-hermes-agent: Designed for Windows users, this zero-install desktop agent comes pre-loaded with 100 tools and support for local models via LM Studio. It eliminates the need for Docker or complex setups, providing a portable GUI-based workflow engine and dynamic tool maker.
entries.xmbshwll/hermes-agent-docker: This project provides a streamlined Docker sandbox image that includes the official Hermes installation and mini-swe-agent. It is built for simplicity, allowing users to maintain persistent state through mounted directories and target specific Hermes versions using build arguments.
entries.jpalmae/hermeshq: HermesHQ acts as a Docker-first control plane for orchestrating multiple agent instances with distinct identities and workspaces. It is unique for its hierarchy-aware delegation features and enterprise MCP access, enabling complex task scheduling across an agent fleet.
entries.JackTheGit/hermes-autonomous-server: Targeting production-ready headless deployments on Ubuntu and Debian, this project configures the Hermes Gateway as a reboot-safe systemd service. It is specifically optimized for autonomous execution of recurring AI cron jobs and integration with the Nous Portal.
entries.ellickjohnson/portainer-stack-hermes: This deployment stack integrates Hermes with a ttyd web terminal for browser-based access and manages the lifecycle via Portainer. It automates the entire setup process through GitHub Actions and Docker Compose, making it highly accessible for container-managed infrastructure.

entries.builderz-labs/mission-control: A self-hosted orchestration platform that manages multi-agent workflows across frameworks like CrewAI and LangGraph. It features a zero-dependency SQLite backend and the Aegis review system for human-in-the-loop task sign-off.
entries.swarmclawai/swarmclaw: This framework enables the creation of autonomous agent swarms with durable memory and the ability to learn skills at runtime. It supports over 23 LLM providers and utilizes visual org charts to manage team delegation.
entries.howdymary/hermes-agent-metaharness: A meta-optimization tool designed to refine agent harness code through automated outer-loop searches. It orchestrates TBLite and TB2 benchmark evaluations to track and improve performance frontiers.
entries.runtimenoteslabs/gladiator: An agent-vs-agent arena where autonomous AI companies compete for GitHub stars. It leverages Hermes Agent and Paperclip to execute tasks, visualizing the competition via a real-time scoreboard dashboard.
entries.1ilkhamov/opencode-hermes-multiagent: A specialized collective of 17 agents organized into research, planning, and infrastructure pipelines. The system ensures high code quality by enforcing mandatory reviewer and tester gates and integrating with MCP servers.
entries.supermodeltools/bigiron: An AI-native SDLC that guides Hermes Agent through an eight-phase development cycle. It uses Supermodel MCP to implement graph-gated phase transitions, ensuring architectural integrity via automated graph-diffs.
entries.Rainhoole/hermes-agent-acp-skill: An ACP-style delegation system that routes tasks between Hermes subagents, Codex, and Claude Code. It maintains clean parent contexts through isolated workflows and enforces safety via character limits and timeouts.

entries.junhoyeo/tokscale: This high-performance Rust TUI tracks token consumption and costs across multiple AI coding environments, including Hermes Agent and Claude Code. It provides local data visualization to help developers monitor their spend and usage patterns across various model providers.
entries.joeynyc/hermes-skins: This utility allows developers to personalize the Hermes terminal interface through YAML-based configuration files for banners, spinners, and ASCII art. Themes can be easily shared and created by inheriting from default settings to support partial skin definitions.
entries.AlexAI-MCP/hermes-CCC: This project ports the Hermes Agent brain and 46 core skills directly into the Claude Code Channel for native execution without external processes or OAuth. It specifically includes MLOps tools for vLLM, GGUF inference, and reinforcement learning training.
entries.unmodeled-tyler/vessel-browser: Vessel is an AI-native browser designed for autonomous control via MCP, allowing Hermes Agent to navigate the web with human-in-the-loop approvals. It features automated page schema inference to help agents detect structured entities and forms during live sessions.
entries.Abruptive/Ankh.md: Ankh.md is a framework for creating scoped Hermes Agents that are restricted to specific folders with their own local configurations and tools. It enables seamless switching between global and project-local agents, each with unique memories and custom instructions.
entries.roli-lpci/lintlang: This static linter uses regex and AST analysis to validate agent configurations and system prompts without incurring LLM costs. It identifies seven specific failure modes, such as ambiguity and missing constraints, to ensure prompts meet HERM v1.1 scoring standards.
entries.0xNyk/openclaw-to-hermes: This migration utility streamlines the transition from OpenClaw to Hermes Agent by mapping personas, memories, and skills into the Hermes YAML format. It automates the transfer of complex model configurations and credentials from local environment files.
entries.42-evey/evey-setup: This deployment stack uses Docker to bundle Hermes Agent with LiteLLM, Ollama, and Qdrant for a zero-cost local setup. It comes pre-configured with 29 community plugins and automated routing for free models and local GPU inference.

entries.nesquena/hermes-webui: This zero-dependency frontend provides full feature parity with the Hermes CLI through a clean, three-panel layout for chat, sessions, and file browsing. It offers a lightweight, build-free way to manage workspaces from any web browser or mobile device.
entries.EKKOLearnAI/hermes-web-ui: A comprehensive dashboard focused on multi-channel communication, allowing users to configure and manage Hermes across Telegram, Discord, Slack, and WhatsApp. It distinguishes itself with deep usage analytics for tracking token consumption, costs, and model trends in real-time.
entries.outsourc-e/hermes-workspace: This native web workspace introduces 'Swarm Mode' for multi-agent orchestration alongside an integrated Kanban board for tracking autonomous missions. It serves as a centralized hub for managing chat, terminal access, memory browsing, and skill deployment.
entries.xaspx/hermes-control-interface: A self-hosted gateway designed for multi-agent management, enabling users to start, stop, and configure individual profiles from a single dashboard. The interface features collapsible tool-call cards and detailed system metrics to monitor the health of the Hermes stack.
entries.dodo-reach/hermes-desktop: Built specifically for macOS, this native application connects to Hermes hosts via SSH to manage remote sessions, cron jobs, and Kanban boards. It provides a high-performance desktop environment for users who prefer native terminal access and local alias management.
entries.clawvader-tech/hermes-telegram-miniapp: This mobile-first React dashboard brings Hermes into the Telegram ecosystem as a Mini App, featuring a terminal-style chat with slash commands. It allows users to spawn background agents and monitor system health gauges directly from their mobile devices.
entries.aivrar/portable-hermes-agent: A zero-install Windows GUI that enables local model execution via LM Studio without requiring admin rights or Docker. It stands out with over 100 built-in tools for TTS and ComfyUI, plus a dynamic 'tool maker' for creating custom API wrappers.
entries.fathah/hermes-desktop: This desktop companion simplifies the initial setup process with a guided installer for both local and remote backends. It provides a unified management interface for 16 messaging gateways and 14 distinct toolsets, including memory systems and personas.
entries.sanchomuzax/hermes-webui: A monitoring-focused dashboard that provides real-time visibility into session activity, gateway status, and cost tracking. It features a powerful FTS5-powered search for inspecting conversation history and offers read-only management of core configuration files.
entries.Euraika-Labs/pan-ui: A chat-first workspace that emphasizes security and extensibility through tool approval cards and an integrated marketplace for MCP servers. It utilizes isolated profile management to keep sessions, memory, and API keys strictly separated.
entries.pyrate-llama/hermes-ui: Featuring a modern glassmorphic design, this single-file HTML interface is built with React 18 for easy self-hosting. It combines a file browser with inline previews and real-time log monitoring to provide a sleek, transparent view of agent operations.
